page: Microsoft Azure FX16mds_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at a high base clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The configuration includes 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with a massive 336.0 GB of system memory, yielding 21.0 GB of RAM per physical core, and includes 945 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core capabilities, with the Geekbench single-core score and text processing performance ranking in the top 10% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show strong performance in the top 25% tier. For artificial intelligence workloads, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 429.6 tokens per second using a small 135M parameter LLM model. However, multi-core operations, redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput fall within the average middle 50% range, and the Passmark memory score registers in the bottom 25% tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX16mds_v2 offers high resource density, particularly regarding its exceptionally high memory-to-vCPU ratio and high-frequency Intel Xeon processor. This makes it a specialized option where memory capacity and single-core speed are prioritized over raw multi-core scaling. The primary tradeoff is the lower relative multi-core and memory benchmark scores in certain synthetic tests despite the large RAM footprint. This server is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications requiring high single-threaded performance and substantial memory capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure FX16mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine designed for resource-intensive workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, it provides 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores, 336.0 GB of RAM, and 945 GB of local storage. The server exhibits strong single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores placing in the top 10% tier, alongside strong single-threaded compression speeds. While multi-core benchmarks and memory throughput tests generally align with mid-range averages, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 21.0 GB per core offers excellent resource density for memory-bound tasks. The FX16mds_v2 is highly suited for large-scale databases, in-memory data stores, and enterprise applications requiring high single-threaded CPU performance combined with massive memory capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX16mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 336 GB of RAM, and 945 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier single-core CPU performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX16mds_v2 is a memory-optimized VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 336 GB RAM, and 945 GB local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-intensive virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and substantial local storage for demanding database workloads.

336.0 GB of RAM providing a high 21.0 GB per core memory density
945 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Best suited for memory-intensive databases and in-memory caching
